new_classes.label_en: Still Wine of Fresh Grapes in containers ≤ 2 L with high total dry extract (CN 2204 21 78)
new_classes.label_de: Ruhiger Wein aus frischen Weintrauben in Behältern ≤ 2 L mit hohem Gesamttrockenextrakt (KN 2204 21 78)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes (not sparkling, not semi-sparkling) presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, with a total dry extract (non-volatile matter including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colorants and salts) meeting the threshold specified in Additional Note 4 to Chapter 22, and not bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), falling under CN subheading 2204 21 78.

A quality of a wine or grape must that quantifies the sum of non-volatile dissolved substances (including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colorants and salts) remaining after evaporation of water and alcohol, expressed in grams per litre, as defined in Additional Note 4 A to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for codes 2204 21 11–98 refers to Additional Notes 4 and 5 of Chapter 22, which define total dry extract thresholds that discriminate the sub-codes 11/18/22/26/32/34/38/42/46/48/57/61/62/66/67/68/71/74/75/76/77/78/79/80/81/82/83/84/90/94/96/98. The new class StillWineSmallContainerHighTotalDryExtract correctly captures: (1) wine of fresh grapes as parent; (2) still (not carbonated); (3) container ≤ 2 L via SmallContainer part; (4) possession of a TotalDryExtractQuality; (5) absence of PDO; (6) absence of significant excess pressure (excluding semi-sparkling). However, the exact numeric threshold for 'high' total dry extract that defines this specific sub-code 22042178 relative to its sibling sub-codes (e.g. 22042177, 22042179) cannot be expressed without the full text of Additional Note 4A specifying the precise g/L boundary applicable to 22042178. The TBox currently lacks a named individual for the exact threshold, so the range restriction on totalDryExtractGPerL is left open. Additionally, Additional Note 5 content (concerning special categories or regions) is not available in the provided legal text and cannot be covered. Coverage is therefore partial.
